data_sources: Granola (GLOC, CLS Health, Plymouth Bay Ortho — meeting notes recovered)

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — unresponsive since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; Osvaldo was not the decision maker, providers never engaged

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Solv passed 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; outside ICP; price-sensitive

excluded_deals.name: Total Primary Care
excluded_deals.reason: Cut off 4/7 — won't replace CTO-built scheduler; Primary Care Connect deal not on Monarch roadmap for 6–9 months

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.40/visit (ClearPay) at scale; 2 pilot locations (4 total); 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition; ~2K calls/week
deals.status_narrative: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27 — first true Monarch win. Jim (CEO) drove it across the finish line. Pricing locked per contracting submission (4/23): pilot phase free; post-pilot $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.40/visit (ClearPay). Contract start date 5/1. Running at 2 locations initially; Katie confirmed 2-location pilot scope. Internal EPD kickoff with Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, and Carli being scheduled. In-person design partner collaboration in Michigan planned. Deal channel renamed to #mon-partner-gloc.
key_risks: Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
No ortho reference customer — Jim flagged this throughout but softened over time
Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity elevated
deals.next_step: Schedule internal EPD kickoff (Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, Carli) then external first meeting with Jim. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: SIGNED 4/27; first Monarch win; internal EPD kickoff scheduling in progress.

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: Pricing TBD pending revised proposal; ~250 providers total (Houston TX); 15–30 providers and ~15–30 telemed visits/day to start; inbound lead originated 4/9
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (CMA, internal champion); Ammar Baba (Director, exec sponsor); CLS President engaged and applying internal pressure
deals.status_narrative: Two-part deal as of Teresa's 4/28 clarification: (1) UC Telemed — green if Solv does not need to read the schedule; (2) Specialties — Kapeesh scoping call needed. Ideal outcome: sign UC telemed + design partner for specialties. Acceptable fallback: UC telemed only. CLS came back 4/24 with pricing asks framed as 'wanting to make it easier to sign': per-encounter or hybrid pricing, 6-month initial term vs. 1 year, waived $2K implementation fee. On 4/29 Brantley drafted a follow-up email to Abigail and Ammar proposing ramp pricing and a design partner path for Telehealth; Teresa approved direction — ramp pricing for UC only, with much better incentives as a design partner for specialties. Executive call with Solv COO/CPO being set for next week (week of May 4). Target launch: June 1.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — this single question gates the entire Part 1 green-light
Pricing concession stack (ramp, 6-month term, waived impl fee) needs finance sign-off and compresses margin
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Send updated proposal to CLS with ramp pricing (UC telemed) + design partner framing (specialties). Schedule COO/CPO executive call for week of May 4. Owner: Brantley/Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Immediately.
deals.next_step_timing: Proposal immediate; exec call week of May 4
deals.key_notes_one_line: Ramp + design partner approach approved 4/29; exec call with COO/CPO being set for week of May 4.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused — was near Paper Process)
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating; Dr. Basmajian (CEO, ultimate DM — never directly engaged by Solv)
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — with no timeline given. Deal was at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign ready to send, 3-phase contract structure modeled at ~$0.60–$1.00/resolved call for ~20K calls/month) before the Assort blocker surfaced 2/25. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv. Pricing range discussed was $300-500/location/month + $1-2/resolved call (with platform fee) or $2-4/resolved call without. Brantley on a passive ~2-week check-in cadence; last touch targeted ~4/14 is now overdue.
key_risks: Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path forward
Check-in now overdue; if Assort resolves and a competitor has stayed active, deal is at risk
Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been directly engaged by Solv
deals.next_step: Brantley to send check-in — targeted 4/14 cadence is overdue. Passive monitoring only; do not push. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return when clear; check-in overdue.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Grey Monarch — ownership unclear)
deals.size: 3 existing ortho locations + 1 standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1; ECW; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user); Christopher LaFave (Exec Director); Eileen Bridenbecker (Ops Manager)
deals.status_narrative: Warm inbound from a Solv-familiar Dir. of Marketing building out a new standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1. Strong April 12 second call with 4-person exec team — bought into online scheduling, want to move fast. Products initially scoped as Experience + Engage; Maya and ClearPay possible but secondary. Stalled on two issues: (1) ECW integration demo not yet delivered (overdue since ~April 14); (2) Heather deprioritized April 21 as 'not at Monarch' but Teresa same day called it a 'grey Monarch deal' worth winning for upsell path into specialties. No activity since April 22. Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to other 3 ortho clinics. Deal ownership between Brantley and a potential hand-off is unresolved.
key_risks: Deal ownership unresolved — Heather said deprioritize, Teresa wants to pursue
ECW integration demo still outstanding, gating their decision
Referral workflow gap blocks the upsell path to other 3 ortho clinics
June 1 UC opening creates urgency — if Solv can't move, they'll find someone else
deals.next_step: Teresa and Brantley to align on deal ownership and whether to pursue as grey Monarch. If yes, deliver ECW integration materials and schedule follow-up call immediately. Owner: Teresa/Brantley. Due: This week — prospect has June 1 launch date.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Ownership conflict (Heather deprioritized, Teresa wants as grey Monarch); ECW demo overdue; June 1 urgency.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; SoCal; ClearPay only
deals.ehr: ECW (inferred from existing Solv relationship context)
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model does not fit a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-up requests since 3/24 — dark for 5+ weeks. Real collection pain exists (post-visit statements) but the product-fit gap is owner-level and genuine. Team is not pushing hard given Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care.
key_risks: Product-fit objection is explicit and owner-level — not a pricing or timing issue
Dark since mid-March; 5+ weeks of ghosting
Aggressive pursuit risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership
deals.next_step: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Re-engage only if ClearPay model evolves to better fit recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection since 3/16; near-dead; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.

early_stage_watch.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
early_stage_watch.stage: Prospecting — board internal meeting ~4/29–4/30; reconnect with Brantley this week
early_stage_watch.contact: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former CEO of AFC)
early_stage_watch.notes: Post-UCA warm intro via Heather at happy hour. First substantive call 4/24 with Brantley. Urgent ortho model; 4 locations. Cash-constrained: opened 3 locations at once; Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling badly. Randy meeting with OS1 team ~4/29–4/30 to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Evan Cory preparing Maya and ClearPay collateral. Reconnect with Brantley expected this week (~5/1–5/5). Cash-strapped flag: could kill the deal before it starts. Monthly board cadence flagged as slow.

executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Closed Won | Design Partner
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.40/visit (ClearPay); 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs; ~2K calls/week
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: James Stilley (CEO)
- **Status**: GLOC signed on 4/27 — first true Monarch win. Contract start date 5/1, 2 locations in pilot. Pricing locked: free during pilot, then usage-based at scale. Internal EPD kickoff with Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, and Carli being scheduled; external kickoff with Jim to follow. In-person collaboration in Michigan planned.
- **Key risks**:
  - Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim raised this but softened
  - Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity elevated
- **Next step**: Schedule internal EPD kickoff and external kickoff with Jim. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo + Design Partner
- **Size**: TBD pending revised proposal; ~250 providers; 15–30 visits/day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops); Ammar Baba (Director, exec); CLS President engaged
- **Status**: Ramp pricing + design partner approach approved by Teresa 4/29. Brantley drafting updated proposal for CLS. Two-part structure: (1) UC telemed (ramp pricing), (2) Specialties as design partner. Executive call with Solv COO/CPO being set for week of May 4. Target launch June 1.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — single question gates Part 1 entirely
  - Pricing concession stack compresses margin; needs finance sign-off
  - EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term flexibility
- **Next step**: Send updated proposal (ramp + design partner); set COO/CPO exec call for May 4 week. Owner: Brantley/Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Immediately.
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and proactive
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — no timeline. Was near-Paper Process before this. Jon confirmed intent to return when clear. Check-in cadence now overdue.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort exit fully outside our control — no active path forward
  - Check-in overdue; competitive exposure if Assort resolves and another vendor is active
  - Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been engaged by Solv
- **Next step**: Brantley to send check-in this week. Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley.
executive_summaries.plymouth_bay_ortho:
**Plymouth Bay Ortho** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Grey Monarch) | New Logo
- **Size**: 3 existing ortho locs + 1 new UC opening June 1; ECW
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, former Solv user)
- **Status**: Heather deprioritized April 21 but Teresa called it a 'grey Monarch deal worth winning.' ECW demo not yet delivered. No activity since April 22. June 1 UC launch creates urgency — if Solv can't move this week, they'll find another vendor.
- **Key risks**:
  - Deal ownership conflict unresolved between Heather's deprioritize and Teresa's pursue
  - ECW demo overdue, gating their decision
  - June 1 urgency creates hard deadline
- **Next step**: Teresa and Brantley align on ownership; if yes, deliver ECW demo materials ASAP. Owner: Teresa/Brantley. Due: This week.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; ClearPay only
- **EHR**: ECW (inferred)
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Admin, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back explicitly on 3/16 — '90% of patients recurring, doesn't make sense.' Dark since mid-March. Pursuing risks the Mederi UC partnership.
- **Key risks**:
  - Owner-level product-fit objection — not pricing or timing
  - 5+ weeks of ghosting
  - Pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership
- **Next step**: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Owner: Brantley.

leadership_table.key_notes: SIGNED 4/27; first Monarch win; kickoff scheduling this week.
Ramp + design partner approach approved 4/29; COO/CPO exec call being set for May 4 week.
Assort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return; check-in overdue.
Ownership conflict; ECW demo overdue; June 1 launch creates hard deadline this week.
Owner's explicit product-fit objection; near-dead; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.
Post-UCA board intro; OS1 internal board review ~4/29; reconnect with Brantley this week.
